reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate analysis: All 6 gates passed - code is reachable (registered in handlers/index.ts:56-58), attacker-triggered via P2P DataColumnSidecarsByRangeRequest, code verification confirms Phase 03's analysis (availableColumns preserves requestedColumns order via filter at dataColumnResponseValidation.ts:75, no sorting applied), spec cross-reference to 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json confirms requirement 'data_column_sidecars_by_range sidecars must be sent in (slot, column_index) order.' Severity downgrade rationale: Impact is limited to interoperability failures with strict P2P clients that enforce ordering. Does not enable slashing, consensus splits, state corruption, or network-wide impact. Affected clients can retry with another peer. Per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json severity thresholds, this does not meet Low severity criteria (>0.01% network impact) and qualifies as Informational (code quality/interoperability issue without direct security risk). Original 01e severity was LOW with bug_bounty_eligible=false and exploitability=indirect-attack, consistent with downgrade to Informational.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: data_column_sidecars_by_range sidecars must be sent in (slot, column_index) order. (forall resp, i < j: resp[i].slot < resp[j].slot || (resp[i].slot == resp[j].slot && resp[i].col < resp[j].col))
